title: Although We Try Our Best
subtitle: 犯错为何能提升创造力？高手都在用的学习机制

paragraphs.en: Although we try our best, sometimes our paintings rarely turn out as originally planned. Changes in the light, the limitations of your painting materials, and the lack of experience and technique mean that what you start out trying to achieve may not come to life the way that you expected. Although this can be frustrating and disappointing, it turns out that this can actually be good for you.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管我们尽了最大的努力，但有时我们的画很少能达到最初的计划。 光线的变化、绘画材料的限制以及经验和技术的缺乏意味着你开始尝试实现的目标可能不会以你期望的方式实现。 尽管这可能令人沮丧和失望，但事实证明这实际上对您有好处。

paragraphs.en: Unexpected results have two benefits: you pretty quickly learn to deal with disappointment and realise that when one door closes, another opens. You also quickly learn to adapt and come up with creative solutions to the problems the painting presents, and thinking outside the box will become your Second nature. In fact, creative problem-solving skills are incredibly useful in daily life, with which you're more likely to be able to find a solution when problem arises.
paragraphs.zh: 意想不到的结果有两个好处：你很快就会学会如何应对失望，并意识到当一扇门关闭时，另一扇门就会打开。 您还可以快速学会适应并针对绘画所呈现的问题提出创造性的解决方案，跳出框框思考将成为您的第二天性。 事实上，创造性解决问题的能力在日常生活中非常有用，当问题出现时，你更有可能找到解决方案。
